title: clerodendrum manombense
intro: Clerodendrum manombense, also known as the Madagascar glory bower, is a flowering plant native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Its leaves and stems possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off bacterial infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use, with some cultures also using it in traditional remedies for various ailments.
